steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank begins the second sentence: '______ in the borough's Upper West Side neighborhood, these speakers are...' The first sentence placed the speakers in Manhattan. The blank must logically connect that information by specifying where in Manhattan — it needs to mark a location, not express similarity, cause, or an example.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Likewise,': signals similarity between two things. No second group is being compared here. ✗
  • Choice B — 'There,': a place adverb pointing back to Manhattan, introducing the more specific location (Upper West Side). Logically and grammatically fits. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Consequently,': signals a result or effect. Nothing in the first sentence causes the speakers to be located in the Upper West Side. ✗
  • Choice D — 'For example,': introduces an illustration of a general claim. The first sentence makes no general claim that the second sentence would illustrate. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
